data.title: Akero Therapeutics reports Q2 2025 earnings with $1.1B cash; Phase 2b trial shows cirrhosis reversal due to MASH. CEO highlights strong results & future trials. R&D expenses increase to $69.3M.
data.description: Akero Therapeutics Inc. reported significant progress in treating metabolic diseases with their drug Efruxifermin (EFX) as per the 96-Week Phase 2b SYMMETRY trial. They highlighted substantial financial strength with $1,086.2 million in cash and equivalents at the end of June 2025. The trial data, published in the New England Journal of Medicine, showed statistical reversal of cirrhosis due to metabolic dysfunction-associated steatohepatitis (MASH) and the anti-fibrotic activity of EFX. The company's President and CEO, Andrew Cheng, emphasized the positive momentum and expected Phase 3 trial results by 2026 and 2027. The EASL Congress 2025 presentations highlighted EFX's potential in reversing cirrhosis in high-need MASH subgroups. The Phase 2b HARMONY study illustrated EFX's effectiveness in improving fibrosis in pre-cirrhotic MASH stages. The financial report for the second quarter indicated promising growth with increased research and development expenses, showcasing Akero's commitment to advancing MASH treatments. Akero Therapeutics aims to address the high unmet medical need associated with MASH and is conducting multiple Phase 3 studies with EFX. Their overall mission is to provide transformative therapies for serious metabolic diseases.

data.title: Tempus AI beat Q2 estimates with a 22 cent loss per share and $314.64 million revenue, up 89.6% YoY. Genomics revenue grew 115.3% to $241.8 million. Hereditary testing sales reached $97.3 million, up 33.6% YoY. Data and services revenue hit $72.8 million, up 35.7% from last year.
data.description: Tempus AI Inc. (NASDAQ: TEM) reported their second-quarter financial results, with an adjusted loss of 22 cents per share, surpassing analyst expectations of a loss of 24 cents per share. Their quarterly revenue was reported as $314.64 million, exceeding consensus estimates of $296.85 million, marking an 89.6% year-over-year increase. Genomics revenue stood at $241.8 million, growing by 115.3% year over year. Oncology testing generated $133.2 million in revenue, showing a 32.9% increase with around 26% volume growth. Hereditary testing sales reached $97.3 million, marking a 33.6% year-over-year increase, with a growth of around 32% in volume. Data and services revenue totaled $72.8 million, showing a 35.7% growth compared to the same quarter of 2024, led by Insights (data licensing).

data.title: Draganfly Inc. will hold shareholder call on Aug. 11, 2025, at 5:30 PM EST to discuss updates. Stock symbols: NASDAQ: DPRO, CSE: DPRO, FSE: 3U8A.

(NASDAQ: DPRO; CSE: DPRO; FSE: 3U8A), a renowned developer of drone technologies, revealed its plans to conduct a shareholder update call on August 11, 2025, at 5:30 PM EST. This update call aims to provide valuable information to investors and stakeholders.

data.title: UnionPay International partners with IHG for up to 15% discount in China for global UnionPay users.
data.description: On August 8, 2025, UnionPay International (UPI) disclosed a new partnership with InterContinental Hotels Group (IHG) to introduce an exclusive promotion. This promotion grants a significant instant discount of up to 15% in China to all global UnionPay cardholders. This collaboration showcases UPI's position as a prominent player in the payment industry, aiming to provide valuable benefits to customers enjoying their stay at IHG properties in China.

data.title: ParaZero's DefendAir system successfully completes field trial, expanding drone defense capabilities.
data.description: In Kfar Saba, Israel, on August 8, 2025, ParaZero Technologies Ltd. (Nasdaq: PRZO) declared the completion of a field trial for a new version of its DefendAir system. The company, known for its innovations in smart autonomous solutions for the aerospace defense sector, showcased an upgraded variant aimed at bolstering its drone defense capabilities. This development represents a notable step forward in ParaZero's efforts to enhance protection in the global manned and unmanned aerial systems (UAS) industry.

data.title: Disregard news release about Kuke Music & Naxos partnership reshaping classical music future with AI, blockchain, and RWA.
data.description: A press release about Kuke Music Holding Limited partnering with Naxos to launch the "Music LEGO Engine" and revolutionize classical music with AI, blockchain, and RWA should be disregarded. The release was issued on August 6, 2025, at 09:15 AM EDT. The partnership aimed at reshaping the future of classical music.

data.title: Human Papilloma Virus Testing Market to reach USD 3.62 billion by 2033 with CAGR of 10.11%. Rising awareness and technology fuel growth.
data.description: A recent report titled "Human Papilloma Virus Testing Market Size and Share Analysis - Growth Trends and Forecast Report 2025-2033" indicates significant growth in the Human papillomavirus (HPV) Testing Market. In 2024, the market size was USD 1.52 billion, and it is projected to reach USD 3.62 billion by 2033. The compound annual growth rate (CAGR) from 2025 to 2033 is estimated to be 10.11%. This growth is attributed to factors such as the rising awareness of cervical cancer, the expansion of screening programs, and advancements in diagnostic technologies.

data.title: NHC reports Q2 2025 earnings: $374.9M in revenues, up 24.7% from Q2 2024's $300.7M. Same-facility net operating up 9.6%.
data.description: In Murfreesboro, Tenn., National HealthCare Corporation (NYSE American: NHC), the oldest publicly traded senior health care company in the US, reported that net operating revenues for the quarter ending June 30, 2025 reached $374,910,000, up from $300,658,000 in the same period in 2024, marking a significant 24.7% increase. The rise in revenues was mainly driven by a 9.6% growth in same-facility net operations during the second quarter of 2025 compared to the same quarter in 2024.

data.description: In a recent report released, it was revealed that the unemployment rate in the country has reached 5.2%, with an estimated 8.4 million people currently without a job. The report also highlighted a decrease in the GDP growth rate to 2.3%, signaling a slowdown in the economy. Additionally, inflation has risen to 3.5%, leading to concerns about the increasing cost of living for citizens. The government has stated that they are implementing measures to address these economic challenges and are confident in a turnaround in the near future.

data.title: McCoy Global's Q2 2025: Revenue up 21% at $24.1M, net earnings down 56% at $1.4M. Declares $0.025 dividend per share.
data.description: McCoy Global Inc. reported its financial results for Q2 2025, including a 21% increase in revenue to $24.1 million from $19.9 million in Q2 2024. The smartProduct revenue accounted for 58% of total revenue at $13.9 million, up 117% from the previous period. Net earnings decreased by 56% to $1.4 million due to higher stock-based compensation expenses. Adjusted EBITDA was $4.8 million. McCoy made advancements in its Digital Technology Roadmap, securing $11.0 million in contract awards for its smarTR™ system. The corporation announced a quarterly cash dividend of $0.025 per common share. Despite challenges, McCoy's CEO Jim Rakievich remains confident in their long-term trajectory. The company's Vice President & CFO Lindsay McGill noted a growing smartProduct adoption and a total backlog of $24.6 million as of June 30, 2025. Total revenue for H1 2025 was $43.4 million. SmartProduct revenue increased by 119% compared to 2024, accounting for 58% of total revenue. McCoy's gross profit margin improved to 36% for the quarter. G&A expenses were $4.3 million for the quarter and accounted for $7.6 million year-to-date, affected by stock-based compensation and bad debts provisions. Sales and marketing expenses increased due to expanded technical salesforce and marketing efforts.

data.title: Big Rock Brewery Inc. reports 45% increase in second quarter sales volumes, with net revenue up to $16.6 million.
data.description: Big Rock Brewery Inc. reported significant growth in sales volume for the trailing twelve months, with a 39% increase. In their financial results for the three months ended June 30, 2025, compared to the same period in 2024, the corporation saw total sales volumes up 45.3% to 84,116 hl, with net revenue increasing by 34.2% to $16.6 million. Gross margin increased to $5.2 million, and operating income improved to $0.4 million from a loss in the previous year. Net income also increased to $0.3 million, and Adjusted EBITDA rose to $1.0 million. For the six months ended June 30, 2025, compared to the same period in 2024, total sales volumes increased by 47.9% to 152,460 hl, with net revenue up 32.7% to $27.8 million. Gross margin improved to $8.9 million, operating income increased, and net income saw a significant rise to $0.2 million from a loss in the previous year. Adjusted EBITDA also showed a substantial increase to $1.7 million. Sales volumes, net revenue, and other financial figures for both three and six-month periods are detailed in the report. Full details of the story are available on Benzinga.com.

data.title: KKR invests in Med-Metrix with Harvest Partners, supporting RCM solutions. Important numbers: KKR, Harvest, Med-Metrix.
data.description: A prominent global investment firm, KKR, disclosed that their credit funds and accounts played a primary role in providing financing to back the growth investment made by Harvest Partners, LP-managed funds in Med-Metrix, LLC, a prominent provider of tech-driven Revenue Cycle Management solutions. KKR Capital Markets also participated as the Left Lead Arranger and Bookrunner for the deal. The Company's manнуюagements plans for the funds weren't specified, but the figures weren't mentioned.

data.title: Sylvamo (NYSE: SLVM) reports Q2 2025 earnings in line with outlook despite $13 million forex impact. Chairman remains optimistic for stronger second half.
data.description: Sylvamo (NYSE: SLVM), the global paper company, has announced its second quarter 2025 earnings. The company overcame a $13 million negative foreign exchange impact and successfully managed a significant planned maintenance outage quarter in over five years. Jean-Michel Ribiéras, Chairman and CEO, stated that the second quarter earnings met their expectations. An audio webcast discussing the earnings will be hosted at 10 a.m. EDT on investors.sylvamo.com.
